steps.title: Manual J Load Calculation
steps.text: Our technicians near {{landmark}} calculate the exact BTU/tonnage needed for your {{neighborhood}} home based on square footage, insulation, and window area.

steps.title: System Selection & Permitting
steps.text: We help you select high-SEER2 equipment (like {{reg_top_brand}}) and pull all required {{county}} mechanical and electrical permits.

steps.title: Airflow Balancing

seasonal_maintenance.headline: Seasonal HVAC readiness for {{state}} climates
seasonal_maintenance.fall_tips: Check your furnace ignitor and flame sensor before the first {{county}} freeze. A failed sensor is the #1 cause of 'no-heat' calls in {{city}}.
seasonal_maintenance.summer_tips: Clear debris 2ft around your outdoor condenser near {{parks}}. High {{state}} humidity requires maximum airflow to reject heat efficiently.

comparison_guide.title: Central AC vs. Heat Pump: What works for {{city}}?

comparison.details: Standard units are great for basic cooling, but hybrid heat pumps in {{city}} can significantly reduce winter heating costs by using electricity for mild cold and switching to gas/electric backup only when {{county}} temps drop.

hiring_local.headline: Why {{city}} homeowners verify HVAC credentials
hiring_local.body: HVAC installs require EPA 608 certification and often a state mechanical license. {{city}}-based technicians who pull permits in {{county}} protect your warranty and your resale value.

neighborhood_pride.headline: From {{neighborhood}} to {{parks}}: load calculations matter
neighborhood_pride.body: Ceiling height, insulation grade, window area, and sun exposure all change block by block in {{city}}. A Manual J load calculation ensures your system is sized right—not over or undersized.

permitting_logic.headline: Permits and HVAC code in {{county}}
permitting_logic.body: Every equipment changeout in {{city}} requires a mechanical permit and inspection. Unpermitted installs void manufacturer warranties and create issues at {{county}} home sale.

Carrier.value_prop: Variable-speed compressor with Greenspeed intelligence—very efficient in {{state}} mixed climates.

Trane.value_prop: TruComfort variable speed—TXV control responds precisely to {{city}} load swings.

Lennox.value_prop: Highest SEER2 ratings available; best for high-use {{county}} climates and utility rebate stacking.

Rheem.value_prop: Solid mid-tier option; wide parts availability for faster {{city}} emergency repairs.

aoe_hints: Oversized AC units short-cycle in {{city}} humidity, leaving air feeling clammy even at the set temperature.
SEER2 ratings replace SEER starting 2023—compare apples to apples when getting {{county}} quotes.
R-410A refrigerant is being phased out; ask if your replacement unit uses the new R-454B standard.
Dirty evaporator coils reduce efficiency by 30%—annual coil cleaning in {{city}} pays for itself.

faqs.q: What size AC do I need for my {{city}} home?
faqs.a: A Manual J load calculation is required for accurate sizing. In {{city}}, a 2,000 sq.ft. home typically needs 3–4 tons, but ceiling height, insulation, and sun exposure in {{county}} all shift that number.

faqs.q: How much does a new AC installation cost in {{city}}?
faqs.a: A mid-range central AC installation in {{city}} runs $6,500–$11,000. High-efficiency variable-speed systems reach $10,000–$14,000 but qualify for {{state}} utility rebates and federal tax credits.

faqs.q: How long does an AC installation take in {{county}}?
faqs.a: Most single-system changeouts in {{county}} complete in one day. New construction or first-time installs requiring ductwork take 2–5 days.

headline_templates: Furnace Repair in {{city}}: when to fix vs. replace your heating system
{{city}} furnace won't ignite? Common causes and what a {{county}} tech will check
Heating repair guide for {{neighborhood}} homeowners: furnace, heat pump, and air handler

faqs.q: How much does furnace repair cost in {{city}}?
faqs.a: Common furnace repairs in {{city}} range from $150 (ignitor replacement) to $1,200 (heat exchanger). A cracked heat exchanger often means replacement is smarter than repair.

faqs.q: Why is my furnace blowing cold air in {{county}}?
faqs.a: Common causes: dirty filter reducing airflow, failed ignitor, faulty flame sensor, or a tripped limit switch. A {{city}} tech can diagnose in under an hour.

faqs.q: Should I repair or replace my furnace in {{city}}?
faqs.a: If repair cost exceeds 50% of replacement and the unit is over 15 years old, replacement is typically smarter. New units also carry {{state}} utility rebate eligibility.

faqs.q: How much does duct cleaning cost in {{city}}?
faqs.a: Residential duct cleaning in {{city}} typically runs $300–$700 for a standard system. Add $150–$300 for UV light or antimicrobial treatment.

faqs.q: How often should I clean my ducts in {{county}}?
faqs.a: NADCA recommends every 3–5 years. In {{city}}, pets, renovations, mold events, or musty smells are reasons to clean sooner.

faqs.q: Can duct cleaning lower my energy bills in {{state}}?
faqs.a: Yes—clogged ducts force your system to work harder. Cleaning restores airflow and can reduce HVAC runtime in {{city}} by 15–25%.

faqs.q: What smart thermostat works best with my {{city}} HVAC system?
faqs.a: Ecobee is most compatible with variable-speed systems. Nest is ideal for standard {{county}} installs. Proprietary systems like Carrier/Trane require brand-specific communicating thermostats.

faqs.q: How much does smart thermostat installation cost in {{county}}?
faqs.a: Professional thermostat installation in {{county}} runs $75–$200 for labor. The thermostat itself costs $100–$300 depending on the model.

faqs.q: Do I need a C-wire for a smart thermostat in {{city}}?
faqs.a: Most smart thermostats need a C-wire for continuous power. Many {{city}} homes have one; we add an adapter when they don't.

faqs.q: How much does a home energy audit cost in {{city}}?
faqs.a: Professional energy audits in {{city}} run $200–$600. Many {{state}} utilities offer free or subsidized audits—we help identify your eligibility.

faqs.q: What does a home energy audit involve in {{county}}?
faqs.a: Auditors use blower door tests, infrared cameras, and combustion safety analysis to identify air leaks, insulation gaps, and equipment inefficiencies in {{city}} homes.

faqs.q: What rebates are available after a {{city}} energy audit?
faqs.a: {{state}} utilities and federal programs offer rebates for insulation, air sealing, and equipment upgrades. Post-audit rebates in {{county}} can total $500–$5,000+.

TX.logic: {{city}} summers push AC systems to 100% capacity for months. Trane's two-stage and variable-speed compressors maintain comfort without short-cycling through intense {{county}} heat.

FL.logic: Near {{neighborhood}}, salt air degrades standard condenser fins rapidly. Carrier's coastal-rated Infinity units have treated coils and last significantly longer in {{city}}'s marine environment.

NC.logic: {{county}} gets hot summers and cold winters. A dual-fuel heat pump (electric heat pump + gas backup) delivers efficient cooling in summer and cost-effective heat when {{city}} temps drop below 35°F.

CO.logic: High altitude in {{city}} reduces gas combustion efficiency by up to 4% per 1,000 feet. Systems must be de-rated and equipped with altitude-specific gas valves to maintain safety and performance.

conversion.closing_trust: We connect {{city}} homeowners with NATE-certified HVAC pros who provide written load calculations and upfront pricing—no bait-and-switch in {{county}}.
